_note: REFERENCE ONLY. This file is NOT wired into data/manifest.json, so the app will never load it. It exists purely so you can see the exact shape a real exam JSON file needs. Copy an existing file in data/<year>/ as your starting point, not this one.

_fieldNotes.examName: Shown as the exam title on the results page, e.g. 'Incourse Examination 1' or 'Test Examination 2'.
_fieldNotes.examYear: Numeric year, e.g. 2026. Must match the folder name under data/ that this file lives in.
_fieldNotes.academicYear: Groups exams together, e.g. 'Honours 2nd Year'. Must match exactly across exams you want listed under the same year.
_fieldNotes.subjects: One entry per subject. 'code' is the key used to look up marks in each student's 'marks' object, so it must match exactly. 'type' is 'theory' or 'practical' — this still controls SGPA/chart calculations (practical subjects can be excluded from those), it just no longer shows as a badge in the subjects table.
_fieldNotes.credit: Credit hours for the subject, used in SGPA calculation.
_fieldNotes.students: One entry per student. 'marks' keys must match a subject 'code' above. Any subject with no matching key defaults to 0 marks.
_fieldNotes.registration: Used as the unique lookup value when a student searches for their result.

_toAddANewExam: 1) Copy this shape into a new file at data/<year>/<exam-id>.json (drop the _note/_fieldNotes/_toAddANewExam keys). 2) Add one entry for it inside data/manifest.json under the matching year. That's it — no app code changes needed.
